title: Relay: delivery jitter (0-500ms) in write task
description:
What: Add a uniform random 0-500ms sleep before each WebSocket send in the relay's per-client write task, hiding timing correlation between message send and delivery.

Algorithm:
1. Open relay/src/ws.rs
2. Find the write task spawned around line 280: let write_task = tokio::spawn(async move { ... })
3. In the main loop's msg = client_rx.recv() arm, after checking for subscription_active but BEFORE sink.send(Message::Text(...)), add:
   let delay_ms = rand::random::<u64>() % 501;  // 0-500ms inclusive
   tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;
4. Add to imports at top of ws.rs: (rand is already used in tests; need production dep from nie-4v7.1)
5. Do NOT add jitter to the offline message drain loop (lines ~324-342) — those are replays of old messages where jitter provides no privacy benefit
6. Do NOT add jitter to ping frames — only to application messages

Defensive rules:
- The jitter sleep runs inside an async block that owns a clone of the channel; no DashMap lock is held during the sleep (confirmed by architecture analysis)
- rand::random::<u64>() is not cryptographic; this is acceptable for timing jitter (not a security-critical RNG)
- 501ms cap: use % 501 not % 500 to include 500ms as a possible value

Team: standard R+I+T
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-relay passes; cargo clippy -p nie-relay -- -D warnings passes; jitter sleep appears in write task message arm; drain loop has no jitter
